name: ExtensionResearchStudy_Result
title: R5: Link to results generated during the study (new)
purpose:
This extension is part of the cross-version definitions generated to enable use of the
element `ResearchStudy.result` as defined in FHIR R5
in FHIR R4.

The source element is defined as:
`ResearchStudy.result` 0..* `Reference(http://hl7.org/fhir/StructureDefinition/EvidenceReport,http://hl7.org/fhir/StructureDefinition/Citation,http://hl7.org/fhir/StructureDefinition/DiagnosticReport)`

Across FHIR versions, the element set has been mapped as:
*  R5: `ResearchStudy.result` 0..* `Reference(http://hl7.org/fhir/StructureDefinition/EvidenceReport,http://hl7.org/fhir/StructureDefinition/Citation,http://hl7.org/fhir/StructureDefinition/DiagnosticReport)`

Following are the generation technical comments:
Element `ResearchStudy.result` has a context of ResearchStudy based on following the parent source element upwards and mapping to `ResearchStudy`.
Element `ResearchStudy.result` has no mapping targets in FHIR R4. Typically, this is because the element has been added (is a new element).

element.short: R5: Link to results generated during the study (new)
element.comment:
Element `ResearchStudy.result` has a context of ResearchStudy based on following the parent source element upwards and mapping to `ResearchStudy`.
Element `ResearchStudy.result` has no mapping targets in FHIR R4. Typically, this is because the element has been added (is a new element).

element.short: Link to results generated during the study
element.definition: Link to one or more sets of results generated by the study.  Could also link to a research registry holding the results such as ClinicalTrials.gov.
